Thank you for your feedback on Castor's gameplay experience. We will make some improvements to Castor as soon as possible to make his Black Feather gameplay more interesting. The following are the internal changes we are currently making. Please refer to the official announcement for the final result.

- The total amount of all erosions applied is doubled, but the proportion of self-damage bonus provided by the erosion amount is halved. In this way, Castor's own damage remains unchanged, but the erosion becomes more meaningful. Generally speaking, in the late game, it is better to cause a certain amount of damage in each round than to burst out super high damage in a single round. Castor's erosion should be able to change this and make it a valid choice to attack at ordinary times.
- If the Black Feather in your hand is not played in this round, you can get an extra shield at the end of the round, so that not playing the Black Feather is also a valid choice.
- The first Black Feather in each round will provide Castor with 10 points of Madness, so as to encourage him to form a strategy of playing one per round.

Ultimately we want all three strategies for Castor's Black Feather to be viable: play all in a single turn to be a direct damage contributor during special periods, play one per turn to be a stable damage buff, and play as few as possible to be a defensive contributor who makes full use of Black Feather.

Thank you for your feedback on Castor's gameplay experience. We will make some improvements to Castor as soon as possible to make his Black Feather gameplay more interesting. The following are the internal changes we are currently making. Please refer to the official announcement for the final result.

- The total amount of all erosions applied is doubled, but the proportion of self-damage bonus provided by the erosion amount is halved. In this way, Castor's own damage remains unchanged, but the erosion becomes more meaningful. Generally speaking, in the late game, it is better to cause a certain amount of damage in each round than to burst out super high damage in a single round. Castor's erosion should be able to change this and make it a valid choice to attack at ordinary times.
- If the Black Feather in your hand is not played in this round, you can get an extra shield at the end of the round, so that not playing the Black Feather is also a valid choice.
- The first Black Feather in each round will provide Castor with 10 points of Madness, so as to encourage him to form a strategy of playing one per round.

Ultimately we want all three strategies for Castor's Black Feather to be viable: play all in a single turn to be a direct damage contributor during special periods, play one per turn to be a stable damage buff, and play as few as possible to be a defensive contributor who makes full use of Black Feather.

Thank you for your feedback on Castor's gameplay experience. We will make some improvements to Castor as soon as possible to make his Black Feather gameplay more interesting. The following are the internal changes we are currently making. Please refer to the official announcement for the final result.

- The total amount of all erosions applied is doubled, but the proportion of self-damage bonus provided by the erosion amount is halved. In this way, Castor's own damage remains unchanged, but the erosion becomes more meaningful. Generally speaking, in the late game, it is better to cause a certain amount of damage in each round than to burst out super high damage in a single round. Castor's erosion should be able to change this and make it a valid choice to attack at ordinary times.
- If the Black Feather in your hand is not played in this round, you can get an extra shield at the end of the round, so that not playing the Black Feather is also a valid choice.
- The first Black Feather in each round will provide Castor with 10 points of Madness, so as to encourage him to form a strategy of playing one per round.

Ultimately we want all three strategies for Castor's Black Feather to be viable: play all in a single turn to be a direct damage contributor during special periods, play one per turn to be a stable damage buff, and play as few as possible to be a defensive contributor who makes full use of Black Feather.
